text: The New York Appellate Division, Third Department, found that defendant's five appellate filings contained at least 23 fabricated case citations. Document metadata showed the filings originated from a program in the client's name. Despite the defendant denying AI use, the court made an express, unhedged finding of generative-AI misuse — the first reported GenAI sanctions decision at the New York appellate level. Sanctions of $7,500 were imposed on counsel and $2,500 on the client.
ai_system_name: Unnamed generative AI (court finding; filer denied AI use)
deployer: Joshua A. Douglass (defense appellate counsel) and Jean LeTennier (defendant/client)
mitigation_gap: Neither counsel nor client verified AI-generated citations before filing; client apparently ran the AI tool and counsel signed submissions without review.
reliance_or_harm: $10,000 in sanctions ($7,500 on attorney Douglass + $2,500 on LeTennier); appeals affirmed with costs.
